creative_brief: Clean, minimal design. No hero image — let the story breathe. Single green CTA button mid-lower body reading 'Join the waitlist — $5 refundable deposit'. Small-format partner farm logos (Bundarra, Collins, Paris Creek, Rachel's Farm) as a horizontal strip near the bottom for social proof.
subject: Last Tuesday, a farmer called me in tears
preheader: She'd just been told her price was being cut again. Here's what we did.
body:
Last Tuesday morning, a call came through from a cattle farmer outside Kempsey.

She'd just been told by her buyer — a wholesaler who supplies the big chains — that her price per kilo was dropping. Again. Third time in eighteen months. She said she wasn't sure she could keep the farm going past winter.

I sat with that call for a long time.

Because this is the part most people never see. The quiet exit. The farmer who loved the land, did everything right — raised animals on pasture, rebuilt soil, refused shortcuts — and still couldn't make the numbers work. Not because the food wasn't worth it. Because too many hands touched it before it reached you.

That's the system we built FarmThru to replace.

No wholesalers. No middlemen. Farm to hub to you. When you order from FarmThru and collect from our Brookvale hub, the farmer who grew your food gets paid what it's actually worth. And you pay less than you'd expect, because we stripped out every cost that doesn't belong.

This isn't abstract. Here's what it looks like right now:

Bundarra Farm sends us pork belly and fennel salami direct. Collins Wild Caught brings in wild-caught salmon from the South Coast. Paris Creek Farms in South Australia supplies carbon-neutral biodynamic dairy. Rachel Ward's own farm in Kempsey grows regenerative produce that goes straight to our shelves.

Every product traces back to a real person on real land. We'll show you where your food comes from — that's not a slogan, it's a promise printed on every order.

But here's the thing. We can only keep this promise if we grow. More customers means more orders, which means more farmers get a fair price, which means more land gets farmed regeneratively. It's a loop — and right now, you can help us widen it.

We're raising funds through equity crowdfunding on Birchal. For as little as $50, you can own a piece of FarmThru and help us bring this model to more families across Sydney, the Central Coast, and Wollongong.

You can join the waitlist now with a fully refundable $5 deposit to get early access before the campaign opens to the public.

That farmer outside Kempsey? She's one of our newest partners. Her price didn't drop. It went up.

That's what this is for.

This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice. Full details in the disclosure document on Birchal.

Rachel & the FarmThru team
